title: Mariscus fasciculiger
taxonomy: Mariscus fasciculiger is a species within the family Cyperaceae, commonly known as the sedge family. This family is a group of flowering plants that belong to the monocot order Poales. As a member of the Poales order, Mariscus fasciculiger shares characteristics with other plants in this group, such as having a single cotyledon during seed germination. The Cyperaceae family is further divided into several genera, and Mariscus fasciculiger is one of the species within this diverse group. Understanding the classification of Mariscus fasciculiger requires knowledge of its phylogenetic relationships and evolutionary history.
